text: Index 										 	Index and Introduction									 										 	This Excel-Document consists of the following sheets:									 										 		Cover Sheet								 		Fire Certificate Inventory List (FCIL)								 		EN45545 classifications								 		Guideline & Instructions								 										 	Cover Sheet									 	The cover sheet has to be filled in by the user and can be printed as cover sheet for the FCIL									 										 	Fire Certificate Inventory List (FCIL)									 	The FCIL can be used by rolling stock suppliers to identify and qualify their products in respect fire safety based on EN 45545-2.									 										 	EN45545 classifications									 	"In column AK of FCIL there is a drop-down menu to select the classification of the material according to EN 45545-2. Not all classifications might be used for some components. Therefore this drop-down list can be filtered in this sheet by adding or deleting an ""x"" in column A."									 										 	Guideline & Instructions									 	"The sheet ""Guideline & Instructions"" assists the user to fill in the FCIL correctly." Supplier Declaration:																				 		The undersigned company hereby certifies and declares under our sole responsibility:																				 		That the system/components/materials part of the scope of supply have been properly declared in this Fire Certificate Inventory List Template in accordance with the requirements defined in EN 45545-2:2013+A1:2015 and/or EN 45545-2:2020 for verification of combustible materials.																				 																						 		That products requiring certification through fire behaviour test reports have been tested in accordance with the applicable requirements in EN 45545-2:2013+A1:2015 and/or EN 45545-2:2020, test samples tested were representative of the mean production characteristics and the systems / components / materials tested are evident from the test reports. Test reports of all materials that required testing are complete and have been submitted to the customer / vehicle manufacturer.									EN 45545-2:2020											 																						 		That all test reports for combustible materials have been issued by independent test laboratories which are accredited to EN ISO/IEC 17025 for those tests. 																				 																						 		"That justification reports have been provided for products that need to be approved on functional necessity and that the reports have been written in accordance with clause 4.7 of EN 45545-2:2013+A1:2015 and/or EN 45545-2:2020"																				 																						 		Validity of Test Reports - TSI LOC&PAS:2023, clause 4.2.10.2.1.(3) - and OJEU L 222/88 chapter 7.1.1.4 (Trans. period)																				 		"Suppliers to the rolling stock industry are responsible for maintaining valid test reports for their scope of supply. Test reports are valid for 5 years from the date of issue and shall thereafter be renewed by the supplier every 5 years if the products or materials are supplied to ongoing production of railway vehicles. During a transitional period ending on 1st January 2026, it is permitted, as an alternative to material requirements specified in point 4.2.10.2.1, to apply the verification of conformity to the material fire safety requirements using the appropriate operation category from EN 45545-2:2013+A1:2015 In case there is no change in the product characteristics and manufacturing process, and no change in the requirements, it is not required to perform new testing of the product if the supplier declares the conformity of the product in a manufacturer declaration, ""UNIFE Manufacturer Declaration - Fire Behaviour of Supplied Products"" (also refer to TSI LOC & PAS, Clause 4.2.10.2.1: Material requirements, and the associated Application Guide)." Information																																			 FCIL Data		Instruction and comments	Examples	"Mandatory/ Optional"																															 Column	Data Field																																		 B	Identifier	"The identifier column enables static references between different rows in the FCIL sheet. When a reference is made to another row number in the FCIL shall the identifier number be specified and NOT the excel row number. Note that! New rows that are created will not contain any formulas. Hence it is recommended that a existing row with formulas are copied and pasted onto the newly created row. Thereafter can data either be entered or changed. "	In the example below shall the identifier number 0251 be specified on the new row that was inserted	Mandatory																															 C	Revision	"Specify the revision number of the document in the column when: • The data was entered for the first time • The data that previously was entered have been updated or altered This column is used in order to ease the review process for the customer or vehicle manufacturer by providing a mean of identifying the changes made since the last supplied revision."	"In the example below a new row was inserted with ID 0251 and then the data on the row with the ID 0003 was updated. The revision numbers is thus changed to _2 as this is current revision number of the document when the FCIL document was updated. As the data on the other rows have not changed since the data were entered the revision number remains at _1."	Mandatory	,																														 D-L	BOM Level	"Enter the BOM level of the assemblies/products/parts that shall be declared in column C. Do not enter any data in column E to L, as those columns are filled in automatically depending on the number entered in column B. Note that the BOM structure used in the FCIL template is the same as used in the UNIFE Material Declaration Template. The assemblies, subassemblies, products and materials that are part of the scope of supply shall be listed in a hierarchical top-down structure in the BOM. The relation between the listed assemblies, subassemblies and products is indicated with the BOM level."	"The top-down structure best reflects the different compliance alternatives that are available, especially when containment rules or grouping rules are applied. In the illustration to the right an example can be seen where a standalone product and an assembly are illustrated. "Example of how to interpret the grouping rules: In the example the following assumptions are made: Product A, B, C and D are interior non-listed products with 50 grams of combustible weight per product and the exposed area of each product is below <0,2m2. Product A, B, and C are considered grouped as the assessment criteria “horizontal distance < 20 mm and vertical distance < 200 mm” is true. The total combustible weight of the group exceeds 100 grams and hence one of the products must be tested. Because the weight of the group is below the 500 gram threshold one of the products shall be tested against requirement set R24 in the standard. If the result of the tested product is compliant to the required hazard level, no further requirements apply to the rest of the products in the group as the untested combustible weight is 100 grams or below. No requirements apply to Product D as the criteria “horizontal distance < 20 mm and vertical distance < 200 mm” is not fulfilled and the combustible mass of the product is below 100 grams. In the table enclosed an example can be seen for how the grouped products shall be filled in: Note that the total combustible weight for the grouped products is listed on a separate row in order to easier verify that the mass is below the thresholds specified in the standard. Also note that the hazard level met is specified for the interior grouped product that was tested against requirement set R24. In the example is Product B tested compliatn against Requirement Set 24. Due to this can Product A and Product C be excluded from testing."	Mandatory if grouping rules are used. The fire barrier properties met shall be specified in column AG and the enclosed volume shall be specified in the “comment/remark” column. Additional information as fulfillment of a particular IP-classification can also be specified therein. If a supplier requests a derogation based on functional necessity arguments, a justification report shall be written in which the supplier meets the requirements specified in clause 4.7 ""Products to be approved on functional necessity"". The article number of the report shall be specified in column AH and the customer expects that the products/materials are tested in end use condition, unless the general performance of the materials used can be specified and explained, as for natural rubber for example."
